FT_IC1.evidence: ABCD study, USA, 1,036 participants (Mage=12.0, 60.7% male) at two-year follow-up

FT_IC2.evidence: Problematic gaming (VGAQ) is unspecified-platform gaming, excluded per B5. No qualifying smartphone or social media variables present.

FT_IC5.evidence: Original ABCD analysis using connectome-based predictive modeling (CPM) on MIDT fMRI data

FT_EC7_reason: FT_EC7 not applicable; paper fails FT_IC2 (no qualifying SM/smartphone variables)
notes: EXCLUDE: unspecified-platform gaming does not qualify as smartphone or social media exposure per criteria B5. Paper focuses on neural predictors of gaming severity, not SM/smartphone ↔ outcome associations.

FT_IC1.evidence: ABCD study, USA, ~10,000 children aged 9-10 years (Data Release 5.0)

FT_IC2.evidence: Screen time measured as total aggregate across unspecified platforms (television, videos, video games, texting, video chat) without separate per-item coefficients in main analyses. Main results report only total screen time β; represents internet use without device/platform specificity per B5. Fails B2 (only summed/total screen time, no per-item breakdown in main results).

FT_IC5.evidence: Original ABCD analysis using linear mixed-effects models on baseline/~9-10 year data

FT_EC7_reason: FT_EC7 not applicable; paper fails FT_IC2 (unspecified-platform screen time, B2/B5 violations)
notes: EXCLUDE: Main analyses report only total screen time (no per-item breakdown) and lack platform specificity, violating both B2 and B5. Supplementary Table S1 shows device-specific results but main reported outcome is total ST only. Mediation analysis with putamen volume does not rescue IC2.

FT_IC1.evidence: ABCD study, USA, 1,186 participants (mean age 11.96 years, 46.5% female) at 2-year and 4-year follow-up

FT_IC2.evidence: Cyberbullying victimization alone is the independent variable. Per B3 binding rule: Cyberbullying alone → NOT_MET. No separately-measured social media variable with its own coefficient (B4 carve-out does not apply). Single cyberbullying question is the only exposure; no qualifying SM platform use.

FT_IC5.evidence: Original ABCD analysis using linear mixed models on fMRI stop signal task and Child Behavioral Checklist data from T1 (2-year) and T2 (4-year follow-up)

FT_EC7_reason: FT_EC7 not applicable; paper fails FT_IC2 (cyberbullying alone, B3 binding rule)
notes: EXCLUDE: Cyberbullying victimization is the only exposure measured; no qualifying smartphone or social media variable. Per B3, cyberbullying alone does not meet FT_IC2. B4 carve-out (cyberbullying + separately-measured SM) does not apply.
